basics.name: John Doe
basics.title: Senior Software Engineer
basics.summary: Senior software engineer with 8+ years of experience building web applications, internal platforms, and customer-facing products in SaaS environments. Strong track record in TypeScript, React, Node.js, and cloud infrastructure, with consistent results in improving product performance, delivery speed, and developer productivity. Combines hands-on engineering with pragmatic system design and close collaboration across product, design, and data teams.

responsibilities: Built and maintained customer-facing product features across a React and Node.js stack.
Led backend and frontend delivery for workflow automation, analytics, and platform reliability initiatives.
highlights: Reduced average page load time by 38% after redesigning critical dashboard queries, introducing server-side caching, and trimming unused client bundles.
Delivered a self-serve workflow builder used by more than 1,200 accounts within the first year, contributing to a 17% increase in expansion revenue.
Cut production incident volume by 29% by tightening observability, standardizing alert thresholds, and improving release checklists.
Mentored 4 engineers through design reviews and pairing sessions, helping the team shorten median pull request cycle time from 2.6 days to 1.4 days.

responsibilities: Developed product features, APIs, and internal tooling for a B2B analytics platform.
Worked with product managers and designers to ship roadmap items from discovery through production rollout.
highlights: Launched a new reporting module that increased weekly active usage among admin users by 24% within 6 months.
Replaced a legacy CSV import pipeline with a queue-based ingestion service, reducing failed imports by 63%.
Introduced shared TypeScript contracts between frontend and backend services, cutting integration defects during QA by 31%.
Built internal deployment tooling that reduced rollback time from roughly 20 minutes to under 5 minutes.

responsibilities: Supported development of web applications and internal tools for small and mid-sized clients.
Implemented UI components, REST endpoints, bug fixes, and automated tests in an agile delivery environment.
highlights: Shipped reusable React form components that shortened implementation time for new client workflows by about 30%.
Improved test coverage across core modules from 41% to 68% over three release cycles.
Resolved long-standing performance issues in an internal admin tool, reducing average search response time from 1.8s to 700ms.

projects.name: PulseOps Incident Dashboard
projects.description: Operational dashboard for incident visibility, service health tracking, and on-call reporting across engineering teams.
highlights: Unified incident, alerting, and uptime data into one internal product used weekly by engineering and support leadership.
Reduced manual status reporting from about 4 hours to less than 45 minutes per week.

projects.name: Multi-tenant Data Import Pipeline
projects.description: Scalable ingestion service for customer CSV and API imports with validation, retry handling, and tenant-aware processing.
highlights: Lowered failed import rates by 63% and improved support turnaround by exposing clear validation feedback to customers.
Handled peak import batches of more than 500,000 records without blocking the primary application database.

projects.name: Developer Starter Kit
projects.description: Opinionated starter template for TypeScript services with auth, logging, testing, and deployment defaults.
highlights: Packaged common service patterns into a reusable internal reference that accelerated new project setup for side projects and prototypes.
Documented conventions for environment setup, testing, and release automation in a concise engineering playbook.

certifications.name: AWS Certified Developer - Associate
certifications.details: Application development, deployment, monitoring, and security on AWS.

certifications.name: Professional Scrum Master I
certifications.details: Scrum principles, facilitation, and delivery practices for cross-functional teams.
